title: Rootless 'podman rm --force' fails with 'given PIDs did not die within timeout'
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

Sometimes `podman rm --force <container>` fails to remove a running container that once had an active Exec session, but not anymore. Once it fails the first time, the container is marked as `Exited`, but `podman rm --force` continues to keep failing.

```
$ podman ps --all
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                             COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS                      PORTS  NAMES
025abd4217ba  registry.fedoraproject.org/f30/fedora-toolbox:30  toolbox --verbose...  27 minutes ago  Exited (143) 5 minutes ago         fedora-toolbox-30
$ podman --log-level debug rm --force fedora-toolbox-30
INFO[0000] running as rootless                          
DEBU[0000] using conmon: "/usr/libexec/podman/conmon"   
DEBU[0000] Initializing boltdb state at /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod/bolt_state.db 
DEBU[0000] Using graph driver overlay                   
DEBU[0000] Using graph root /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage 
DEBU[0000] Using run root /tmp/1000                     
DEBU[0000] Using static dir /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage/libpod 
DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/1000/libpod/tmp      
DEBU[0000] Using volume path /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes 
DEBU[0000] Set libpod namespace to ""                   
DEBU[0000] [graphdriver] trying provided driver "overlay" 
DEBU[0000] overlay: mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs 
DEBU[0000] backingFs=extfs, projectQuotaSupported=false, useNativeDiff=false, usingMetacopy=false 
DEBU[0000] Initializing event backend journald          
DEBU[0000] using runtime "/usr/bin/runc"                
DEBU[0000] Setting maximum rm workers to 16             
DEBU[0000] Killing all processes in container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 with SIGTERM 
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
WARN[0010] Timed out stopping container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 exec sessions 
DEBU[0010] Killing all processes in container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 with SIGKILL 
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
WARN[0000] no such directory for freezer.state          
DEBU[0015] Failed to remove container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7: failed to kill container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 exec sessions: given PIDs did not die within timeout 
DEBU[0015] Worker#0 finished job [(*LocalRuntime) RemoveContainers func1]/025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 (failed to kill container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 exec sessions: given PIDs did not die within timeout) 
DEBU[0015] Pool[rm, 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7: failed to kill container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 exec sessions: given PIDs did not die within timeout] 
ERRO[0015] failed to kill container 025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7 exec sessions: given PIDs did not die within timeout
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This doesn't happen reliably, but every once in a while, but I believe I only started seeing it with `podman-1.5.0`.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.5.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.7
  podman version: 1.5.0
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.10.1
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.5.0-2.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon

  MemTotal: 16530231296

    package: runc-1.0.0-93.dev.gitb9b6cc6.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc

      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev

  SwapTotal: 8414818304

  cpus: 4

  hostname: bollard
  kernel: 5.2.7-200.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 36m 35.57s

  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com

  ConfigFile: /var/home/rishi/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 1
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /tmp/1000
  VolumePath: /var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

I have only seen this happen on Fedora 30 hosts, possibly because I have only tried `podman-1.5.0` on Fedora 30.

comments.body: @haircommander Hmm. Any thoughts here? Looks like exec session PIDs are not dying, even to a SIGKILL, from our perspective.

@debarshiray I wasn't able to reproduce running:
`podman run -d alpine top`
`podman exec -l sleep 2`
`podman rm -fl`
note: in the second step I tried killing the exec session before removing, as well as letting it terminate gracefully, neither failed. Do you have a better situation I can debug with?

> @haircommander Hmm. Any thoughts here? Looks like exec session PIDs are not
> dying, even to a SIGKILL, from our perspective.

I believe that there were no exec session PIDs to kill here because the exec:ed session was already gone. The sequence of events were:
```
$ podman start <container>
$ podman exec ... <container> /bin/bash
# <ctrl+d out of /bin/bash>
$ podman rm --force <container>
<failed>
$ podman ps --all
<container shows as exited>
$ podman rm --force <container>
<failed>
```

So, by this time the exec session PIDs should be gone, but the container was also left running until the first `podman rm --force` attempt.

> @debarshiray I wasn't able to reproduce running:

Sadly, this doesn't reproduce reliably, but does happen every once in a while.

If you want me to dig up something more, then let me know. I haven't touched the system so at the moment I can reproduce at will. A reboot or something can again make it disappear.
does `podman inspect -l` show any ExecIDs?

```
$ podman inspect --type container --format "{{.ExecIDs}}" fedora-toolbox-30
[6cf2a1302c11ec823b1e88193687a1e3f6d080ef4d575442e5324fedce075c54]
```
and what about the output of `ps -aux | grep conmon | grep 6cf2a1302c11ec823b1e88193687a1e3f6d080ef4d575442e5324fedce075c54`

it seems we have saved an exec session, but I am guessing the pid has already stopped, but we never cleaned up the ExecIDs, so podman fails to kill a pid that is already dead. if nothing comes up for the above command that'll confirm it.

```
$ ps aux | grep conmon | grep 6cf2a1302c11ec823b1e88193687a1e3f6d080ef4d575442e5324fedce075c54

/var/home/rishi/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay-containers/025abd4217ba14e95ceea799cee9e29a0d446b76d08765b724371c3cc3ed67d7/userdata/6cf2a1302c11ec823b1e88193687a1e3f6d080ef4d575442e5324fedce075c54/exec-process-987276411

@haircommander We do ping each PID with signal 0 to make sure it's still alive - I'm pretty sure the PIDs exist.

Do you want me to do some more poking?

I am asking because I will be travelling in a few days and will have to power off the affected system, which can potentially hide this bug and we'd have to wait until it reveals itself again.
does this occur commonly enough to pursue?
It used to be somewhat common enough. Since then I got distracted by the Cgroups v2 issues, so I am not sure right now. I will try to reproduce this one again in the coming days.
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
@debarshiray Since we have had no feedback for a month, I will close this PR.  Please reopen if you want us to continue to look into it, and  you have more feedback.

Hello,

I encounter the same issue. Would it be possible to re-open the issue?

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

"podman rm --force" does not kill the container. Same behavior running the container rootless or not.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  podman run -d  --name wp_db -v /var/tmp/mysql:/var/lib/mysql -e MYSQL_DATABASE=wordpress -e MYSQL_USER=wordpress -e MYSQL_PASSWORD=password -e MYSQL_ROOT_PASSWORD=password-d mariadb

2. podman rm -f wp_db

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: cannot remove container f4a934759ffc96d8d15a3677d88bcd88a2954fd846a817d97166024571c1596b as it could not be stopped: given PIDs did not die within timeout


**Describe the results you expected:**
container is stopped

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

"podman stop && podman rm" work fine.

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**

/kind bug

**Description:**

Command "podman rm --force" does not remove the container (is running). This only happens for rootless containers.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

```
podman create alpine sleep 999
podman start -l
podman rm -f -l
```

**Describe the results you received:**
`Error: cannot remove container 0430f582afed34b74d43891ed8801c0f596d728d5ba9a9d752ecf0debd0d35da as it could not be stopped: given PIDs did not die within timeout`

**Describe the results you expected:**
ID of the removed container + 0 return value

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

This behavior can be reproduced reliably.
Running `podman stop -l && podman rm -l` successfully stops and removes the container.

```

I can pick this one with https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5014. I believe to have a fix.
@vrothberg Did you ever finish this?

I worked on it but could not find any bug in the code. However, we have seen quite frequently in the passed that the default timeout of 10 seconds is exceeded.

Matt suggested to make it configurable in `containers.conf` which sounds like a pragmatic and straight-forward way to address the issue.
